_methodology: cpupower frequency-set -g performance + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, taskset -c 0, nice -20. 5 runs LTO, 3 runs no-LTO. bench_bitcoin -filter=<benchmark> -min-time=3000. Medians computed from all runs.

machine_spec.turbo: disabled (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 + cpupower — hard lock, sudo available)

note: All numbers from Bitcoin Core bench_bitcoin binary — real Bitcoin Core validation pipeline. NOT bench_unified. Hard turbo lock: cpupower + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1. LTO: 5 runs, median. no-LTO: 3 runs, median. err% from nanobench per-run internal measurement.

results_lto.note: Release + LTO (-DCMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION=ON) — recommended production build. 5 runs, hard turbo lock.

VerifyScriptP2WPKH_ns.verdict: Parity — Ultra 0.4% slower, ranges overlap, inconclusive

results_nolto.note: Release without LTO (-O3, no -flto). 3 runs, hard turbo lock. HONEST: Ultra is ~0.5-1.0% SLOWER than libsecp without LTO. PERF-002 reduced the gap from ~1.1% (pre-fix) to ~0.5-1.0% (post-fix) but did not eliminate it. Use LTO for production Bitcoin Core builds.
results_nolto.build: Release (no LTO), GCC 14.2.0. build-ultra-release-nolto vs build-libsecp-release-nolto.

test_suite.note: 749/749 Bitcoin Core unit tests pass with Ultra backend

nolto_gap_root_cause.status: code contributors identified and fixed; any residual footprint claim withdrawn pending a controlled minimal-profile measurement
nolto_gap_root_cause.real_contributors: Two concrete, code-verified contributors to the ECDSA verify path were found and fixed: (1) a redundant y²=x³+7 on-curve check inside secp256k1_ecdsa_verify (removed in c67edc1c / 40697447 — the opaque pubkey struct is already curve-validated at parse, matching libsecp's trust model); (2) ECDSA DER signature parsing, replaced with a fast strict-DER scalar validator in shim_ecdsa.cpp.
nolto_gap_root_cause.withdrawn_claim: A prior speculative root-cause attribution (to instruction-footprint / cache pressure) was withdrawn — it had no perf-counter evidence. The shipped secp256k1 footprint depends on the build profile (the bitcoin-core profile disables FROST/ZK/ECIES/BIP352/etc., and the linker dead-code-eliminates unused symbols), so a clean linked-footprint comparison under the minimal bitcoin-core profile is required before any size claim in either direction.
nolto_gap_root_cause.recommendation: Use Release+LTO for production Bitcoin Core builds. ConnectBlock numbers must be quoted only from a frequency-locked run.

known_limitations: VerifyScriptP2WPKH: parity only (Ultra 0.4% slower, ranges overlap, inconclusive). Not a regression vs libsecp.

Without LTO: ConnectBlock measured ~0.5-1.0% slower than libsecp256k1 in the 2026-05-12 frequency-locked run; cause attribution is under re-measurement (see nolto_gap_root_cause). Use Release+LTO for production.
SignTransactionECDSA libsecp had one outlier run (185µs vs ~162-165µs for others) — median is used which limits impact.
_frequency_sensitivity_note: Ultra ConnectBlock numbers are only meaningful with a locked CPU frequency. At locked 2.5 GHz (cpupower -g performance + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1) the canonical results above apply. Without locking the governor (no_turbo alone), ConnectBlock timings drift run-to-run — those uncontrolled-frequency numbers are a measurement artifact of the unlocked governor, NOT a backend property. Always lock the governor before quoting ConnectBlock. (A prior speculative hardware-cause attribution for the drift has been removed as unsubstantiated.) Signing advantage (SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot) is robust across conditions.

results_uncontrolled_frequency_2026_05_1.note: Measurement without cpupower (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 only, variable 800-2500 MHz). Backend commit: 79363c79. Compiler: GCC 14.2.0 Release+LTO. This is NOT the canonical measurement — use results_lto for performance claims.

ConnectBlockAllEcdsa_ms.verdict: libsecp +7.5% — measurement artifact (no cpupower frequency lock)

ConnectBlockAllSchnorr_ms.verdict: libsecp +2.6% — measurement artifact (no cpupower frequency lock)

results_2026_05_30_parse_cache_samesessi._note: Single-session 4-way (ultra/libsecp x no-LTO/LTO), 3 interleaved rounds, taskset -c0 nice -20, governor=performance, intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, min-time=3000ms, non-overlapping medians. POST parse-cache fix (ShimPubkeyParseCache in shim_pubkey.cpp). ratio=libsecp/ultra (>1 = ultra faster); ultra_pct_faster=(libsecp-ultra)/libsecp.
results_2026_05_30_parse_cache_samesessi._caveat: ECDSA-verify gains (ConnectBlockAllEcdsa, P2WPKH) are partly amplified by the benchmark's pubkey REUSE (ConnectBlock=5 keys, VerifyScript=1 key) hitting the new decompression cache. Real blocks with many UNIQUE pubkeys cache-miss, so the ECDSA-verify margin shrinks toward parity (libsecp also does not cache pubkey parse). Schnorr/Taproot gains are structural (prebuilt GLV) and hold for unique keys.

operation: Bitcoin Core integration benchmark (ConnectBlock / signature verify under the test suite)
claim_scope: Bitcoin Core secondary-backend integration timing with methodology caveats (see _methodology); NOT a standalone microbenchmark or a native-GPU claim
